What is going on everybody? Welcome back to application funnel amplifiers. We are going to do a quick walkthrough of this entire application funnel from beginning to end. So as you build these little pieces individually, you can have the whole picture in mind. Sweet, so this is the sales page for automated intimacy. So our starting point for AFA is where someone decides they want to book a call to explore that opportunity further. So first thing to point out, we did add another call to action on this page. So we have schedule a call, which is our main call to action, but there will of course always be people within your audience who just don't need a call, right? Might even not want a call. And as long as you as the creator, the person fulfilling on the course or the program are comfortable with someone joining without a call. So that's a very important distinction. You may want to qualify someone and make sure that there's someone you want to work with before. But if you feel confident enough in allowing people in without that, what you can always do is give that option below where someone could check out without the call itself. This may work well for the lower end of high ticket programs like AI was in this case, for some folks just, they've read the page, they've been on the list for a while, they're very confident and they just want to move forward and we don't want to add friction to them moving forward. So that's what that's doing there. Yeah, I think that point you said, Ryan, about the feeling comfortable is very important because I felt very comfortable with our audience and the level of comfort we already had with our audience to allow that, but I can imagine some of the personal development offers that I've worked on and currently work on, we would, even if someone was willing to do it, we would choose not to do that because as Ryan said, we'd want to make sure that there's a good fit, not only for them, but for the dynamic that you're looking to create in that space. So yeah, it's definitely a very personal decision, but it is nice to see how you can do that. For sure, and I will say that this page wasn't being sent out to cold traffic, we didn't have ads, cold ads, like driving to this, right? So those who were landing here were likely people that we were already familiar with and those who were warm and familiar with us. So this takes us to the, yeah, basically the application or the booking, you know, we call it the booking call request on this page. So that's where you land here. Like even when folks are excited about a program, like you have to be understanding that not a lot of people love filling out applications, right? And I speak to this in the questions, right? It's kind of absurd how there might be resistant and friction and take a 30 to 45 minutes for an application when the investment in the program is gonna be multiple hours, if not dozens of hours, right? But friction is friction, right? And it's important to justify why you're asking the questions you're asking. So this is really our frame, right? We're committed to that 100% success rate. And we really want to make sure that someone can be a fit before extending an invite. So that's the way I framed it. And of course you can frame it in ways that are meaningful to you and your audience. Yeah, and you'll see that theme, I think, as you go through every step of this funnel. You get so used to going through these applications and first of all, like 80% of people are using the same questions over and over again. Well, it's clear that there's legit reasons for every question we're asking here. Yeah, we're doing it in a bit of a more fun, playful way that probably also helps people want to continue moving through, right? Because they're curious about what's about to be asked because they're so playful. I think that's a nice little trick there. Cool. Let's keep it going. What do you currently perceive as your biggest missed opportunities, leakages, or inefficiencies in your marketing or sales process? So once in a language matters, right? Like if you're specific around a leakage or an inefficiency, that gets people thinking a lot more clearly around simply saying what is your biggest problem around this topic, right? So get a little bit more precise in how you language things and you're gonna get more precise answers, such as, you know, not drinking enough coffee. Good to know. I'm drinking dandelion tea right now as a coffee replacement. So I feel you on that. Good for you, you're ahead of me. And then the mastery question, right? It's true, I do speak a lot about coffee and my lack of coffee. All right. I'm not sure if Phil's gonna be qualified for the program by the end of these questions. We're gonna have to really review these answers and decide if we wanna move forward. Continuing on mastery, what resonates most about the concept of automated intimacy? So this is also a way of qualifying to make sure that they're as warm as we're assuming they are, right? Have they gone through some of the material? Have they read the sales page, right? There we go. So did you speak to this, Ryan, but this is definitely something I speak to when you go through the breakdowns, setting up the application. But we chose for the same reason, related to the same reason why we allowed people to fast track the application as a whole. We chose not to gate people with these transparency alerts. So just as a reminder, as you go through my walkthroughs, depending on your audience, these can be the thing that actually gracefully exit people from this conversation and give them an alternative path versus letting everybody onto the schedule, which again, with our current list size and our comfort with having 90% warm audience, we let anybody schedule, but that's certainly an option as you're building out your application flow. If you are taking your own sales calls or enrollment calls, I would say you should be borderline swiping transparency alert number one. The reason why there are a lot of no shows on sales calls are people are just scared shitless being with a hardcore closer who is gonna pull every trick to get them in. One of the best ways to combat no shows, we had a single no show on any of these calls. And I think the reason is they know what to expect. They know who they are getting on the other side of that Zoom call and that creates a sense of safety and trust. So if you are taking your own calls, don't underestimate the power of being transparent about that and making them aware that you're not here to close them. And there's a whole other conversation about that for another day, but important to note there. Transparency alert number two, we do best with self-sufficient entrepreneurs and business owners who don't have some urgent life or death problem that they need us to save them from. Yes, solving problems is cool, something we're great at. So this is really setting our boundary, making sure that we're all on the same page. And then finally, so the big investment one where people will debate all day about whether or not to include this or not. Once again, it's all about setting up safety on the call. You want people present on that call. You want people feeling safe to be open and vulnerable and true to what is going on in their heads. And if there's that like, that big elephant in the room where they're almost afraid to want that program, where they're almost afraid to express interest because they don't know whether or not their interest is tied to an investment that they may not be ready to make, that creates a gardeness, whether it's a cactus gardeness or an unconscious one who creates gardeness. So since we are taking our own calls since we are not focused on insane volume at this level of scale, I'm cool with this. I wanna make sure that everyone on the other line knows what that pricing range is, is comfortable with it and is still confident in having this conversation to explore whether or not to join. That's the kind of conversation I want to be having as someone who is, I get lack of better word, a co-director of enrollment. I don't consider myself a salesperson. I consider myself a co-director of enrollment. And I want to have conversations with people who are aware of that off the bat. Thank you, Bill. Yeah, totally. I think, you know, and what you'll see here, we're putting a range. In my case, when I built these with clients, if it's a single price, like it's a $5,000 investment, that's what you'll see. It's literally at the $5,000 investment. I think for us, the range, and this is important nuance, it's truly because there are multiple ways you could choose to invest and be supported in this offer. And for me, the range is kind of the balancing act of like, if you were to say, okay, we have three different versions of this offer, and now I'm gonna pull it out all this. Now you're gonna create kind of offer confusion and a little bit of overwhelm around kind of what someone's getting. So I prefer to save all that nuance for the conversation and really zero in what's gonna be most supportive to them. But as Ryde said, very clear, if someone's been paying attention on the sales page, it's very clear what the minimum investment is and the high end. And so I think the range, if you have multiple offer tiers, is a great way to handle that. Yep, and if you do have a range, state what that range is dependent on, right? So that's another element of creating safety. I don't say that it's four to 15K, depending on what I wanna charge you on that day or the money that you have. I say very clearly that it's dependent on the level of one-on-one support you're gonna get. So once again, that creates safety, which is gonna decrease essentially the amount of people ghosting. And then finally, right? Like when you do present information like that, this is just my style, but do so with a degree of lightness, right? When I talk about them being a solid fit, I want them to be able to make that decision very clearly without stomach, or maxing out credit cards, or sacrificing enjoyment of spotting. So this is all once again about building that magnetism that I am truly on their side, which I am. I as a program creator and someone fulfilling it, don't want to be coaching and leading and consulting somebody who has this constant stomach clench and needs to make an ROI on day two, right? That was part of our last transparency alert where we're not saviors. So this is really another way of just reinforcing that. Yeah, and I'm just keeping it light, keeping it fun, with our answers here. Not picking it too seriously. Cool. And then after all that comes, then of course, the basic admin stuff, the personal details, you're essentially gonna get zero drop off on this stuff when someone has already gone through all of that. And then very clear directionality and leadership, right? Do that. And then on the next page, you're gonna be able to book a call with Ryderfield to discuss a perfect fit. I don't know if you covered this at all and there's all sorts of opinions on this as well, but the only other nuance that I'll mention here is that again, we are selling to an audience we're very familiar with, warm, we are not running ads. So putting this step at the end, very comfortable with. If I was running, if this was primarily based on cold traffic, I would have found the way to incorporate this information much earlier in the flow because then I have optionality around how I could potentially follow up with someone who has chosen to kind of not move forward with the full completion. So that's just a bit of a nuance that, as to why this is at this particular phase. That's a good distinction. We weren't using this application to generate leads, right? Yeah, two other things I'll mention here are, we are reinforcing that, where was it, is that transparency alert number one? But essentially where we are very clear that you're meeting with us, this is a reinforcement of that. If they didn't catch it the first time, they're like, oh yeah, I'm meeting with Ryan Phil. The other reason that's important here, we, if you're a solopreneur or you're the only person doing the call, then your flow here can look a little different where it could be your picture, for example, and maybe even a little reinforcing bio that makes it more unfriendly for them to continue to move forward. Because Ryan and I were trying to help each other throughout with the distribution and workload of being a team, and this is even more important if you had a full on sales team. We have kind of a round robin flow set up here. So this is the combined availability of both Ryan and I, and someone who's been working with us is the combined availability of both Ryan and I, and someone who's not actually seeing who they're scheduling with. So that's another reason why it's kind of important to reinforce that at the top there. And you'll see here, I mentioned this in the backend of the application video. This is just a little thing where you'll see it's already prefilled their name and email. People generally don't like being asked to do that multiple times in the same flow. So there's a little bit of trickery involved in that, which I do break down, but if you can pull that off with the tool you're using, usually it will help with completion rates. So the warm traffic flow like this, most people have gotten this far are gonna do it either way. But just a nice thing for your people. Yeah, the last thing I'll really mention is like, take an extra look at the name of the meeting, right? So it was very clearly explore automated intimacy, right? So it's not called a strategy session. It's not called a breakthrough session. It's very clear in its intention so that when folks do come on that call, it's what we're there to do, right? The part here that I am essentially the biggest, most passionate, as you can tell, passionate advocate of is essentially unlocking that journey, right? Section two, unlocked your journey inside automated intimacy. I think one of the biggest resistances and fears people have around joining any kind of higher ticket program is that there's a bit of a leap of faith before going in, right? It might sound great on a call. It might sound great on the sales page. And then you get in there and the first few lessons you start feeling this clinch of like, shit, I just paid money for this, like really? So we wanted to create a trust right off of that, right? We dropped essentially the first two videos inside automated intimacy on this page for two reasons, right? To give that trust, to give that confidence, but also to generate that momentum. Essentially someone is halfway through module one of the program, by the time they have finished this page. Now that is a very, that is a literal sense of momentum. You pretty much said it all. It's from here, it's really just kind of the follow-ups that they'll be receiving after the fact as they kind of go through these various steps. Amazing. So yeah, we have lessons obviously in this program that go over the nuances of both the copy and the tech of the application itself, the questions, the tech behind it, as well as this page. So go through all of that step by step. These lessons are intentionally short and immediately practical and actionable. And I think you will find a lot of lift and a lot of clarity around why you're doing the things you do in an application funnel. It's one of those things that gets patched together really quickly and without much thought, yet it is one of the biggest levers in a coaching or consulting company. It really is the bridge between someone who is a potential leader, a potential customer, and someone who joins your program. So it's worth doing so at the degree of intentionality. So I mean, that's what AFA is about. It's about learning our intention, what we're coaching in these few little assets and giving you the step by step instructions of implementing it. So that's all I got. This is the final meal. This is the finished meal, the casserole, the marketing casserole, if you will. Bill, any closing thoughts? Just one thing, because it dawned on me here that I didn't mention it in my kind of scheduling walkthrough. One little extra optimization tip here in your confirmation email. This doesn't apply so much if you're selling to other marketers and tech people and people that understand this stuff, but having worked in a lot of niches where that's not the case, don't forget that your audience may not be as savvy to Zoom as you are. And so one thing I love doing at this event here is going to be a, if there's going to be a virtual conversation in that confirmation, make sure you give explicit step by step instructions on you will be meeting on Zoom. Here's a quick couple of steps on optimizing for the call. And here's where you can download Zoom if you're not familiar with it. And you might even want to create a little separate page on your website that's like steps to Zoom and put that in there, because I've seen that happen a lot where people assume the whole world knows how to use it. Most of the world is getting there, but you really make for a much more efficient and friendly call where your pre-customer is not feeling flustered when they arrive and you're able to stay on a really nice timely schedule. And instead of spending that time messing with tech here, you're having a much more thoughtful conversation. So just kind of hit me. I thought it might be worth mentioning it there since it was on this page. Make sense. So that is it for this walkthrough, the practical step by step trainings on the copy as well as the tech. Are somewhere below this video as well as the link so that you can walk through this funnel yourself and see how it plays out in real time. And as always, hit us up with questions, right? Inside CTC Circle, if you're a member, light us up with any questions you have along the way.
